world.title: Who are the Australians aboard vessels trying to break Israel’s blockade of Gaza?
world.description: What Juliet Lamont and 14 other Australians, including Bianca Webb-Pullman, Madeleine Habib and Surya McEwen, are about to do has never been more dangerous. They’re terrified, but they won’t stop. Read more.

world.title: Scott Bessent says US holds Hormuz blockade, rejects Iranian or Russian oil waivers
world.description: The United States will not renew sanctions waivers for certain Iranian and Russian oil cargoes. The decision tightens pressure on both producers as energy markets remain strained.

world.title: Beggars in Kuala Lumpur growing more assertive
world.description: A trend is emerging: groups of women with young children are increasingly targeting passers-by. Read more at straitstimes.com.

world.title: Man arrested after booing at Australian Anzac service
world.description: Police confirmed a 24-year-old man was arrested for "an alleged act of nuisance" during the service and said several others were moved on.

world.title: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman "deeply sorry" for failing to alert law enforcement to Canada school shooter's ChatGPT account
world.description: The ChatGPT account of the shooter, who killed eight people in a small British Columbia community, had been banned about eight months prior to the massacre.

world.title: Malaysia appoints new anti-graft chief
world.description: Malaysia names former judge Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman as the new anti-graft chief, taking over from Azam Baki. Read more at straitstimes.com.

world.title: Iran's foreign minister arrives in Pakistan, Trump expects offer satisfying US demands
world.description: Iran says it has no plans to meet US officials, while the White House says special envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner will travel to Pakistan on Saturday (Apr 25) for talks.

world.title: Trump is in ‘no rush’ for an Iran deal, but he can’t wait forever
world.description: With a stranglehold on Iran’s economy, Trump is in a strong position to wait it out. But he doesn’t have the patience for that, and neither do Americans

business.title: Lachy Groom to back India startup Pronto at a $200M valuation, sources say
business.description: This round, should it occur, would double the house-help startup's valuation in a matter of weeks.

business.title: Walmart recalls 50,000 FitRx adjustable dumbbells over injury risk
business.description: Nearly 50,000 FitRx SmartBell adjustable dumbbells sold at Walmart are being recalled after reports of weight plates dislodging during use, posing an impact hazard.

business.title: Blended meat: The food of the future?
business.description: Explore blended meat, a novel food combining animal and plant proteins, and its potential to revolutionise the food industry. Read more at straitstimes.com.

business.title: China Says US Export Bills Risk Disrupting Chip Supply Chains
business.description: China said it is closely monitoring US legislative plans after the House Foreign Affairs Committee advanced export-control bills targeting semiconductors, which Beijing warned could disrupt global supply chains if passed.

business.title: Review: DBS Insignia
business.description: The DBS Insignia packs a punch, but the removal of its dining vouchers and annual fee waiver might prove too much for some cardholders.

science.title: New method to detect 'faintest whispers' of nuclear proliferation from space
science.description: Scientists are developing new advanced radiation detection systems and X-ray sensing techniques to boost nuclear security.

science.title: Why delaying climate action now means higher seas by 2100 - new research
science.description: Imagine your favourite sunny beach. Anywhere will do. You look out and see the ocean stretching to the horizon. To a glaciologist, that view is not just water; it’s melted ice. Our new study shows that the best case sea-level rise scenarios may now be out of reach. Around 20,000 years ago, during the most […]

science.title: JWST Hunts for an 'Earth-Moon' Twin in a Habitable Zone, But the Star Has Other Plans
science.description: The Moon has played a huge role in the development of Earth. It stabilizes the planet, tempered dramatic climate swings, and possibly even provided the tidal heating that might have led to the first life forms. So it’s natural we would want to find a similar Earth/Luna system somewhere else in the cosmos. But astronomers have been searching for one for years at this point to no avail. And a new paper from Emily Pass and her colleagues at MIT, Harvard, and the University of Chicago describes using the James Webb Space Telescope to track some of the most promising exomoon candidates - only to be foiled by the star they were orbiting.

science.title: NASA sets three priorities for US space strategy: Moon return by 2028, lunar base and commercial expansion
science.description: Washington, April 25 (IANS) NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman has said that the space agency has three core priorities —returning astronauts to the Moon by 2028, building a sustained lunar base, and expanding commercial partnerships in low-Earth orbit—as part of a broader push to reshape US space strategy.

science.title: Scientists revive 24,000-year-old organism from Siberian permafrost
science.description: A 24,000-year-old rotifer frozen in Siberian permafrost was revived and resumed normal biological functions, including reproduction, according to a study in Current Biology.

science.title: Scientists revive ancient 24,000-year-old ‘zombie worm’ from Arctic ice - then it reproduced
science.description: Scientists revived a 24,000-year-old microscopic organism frozen in Siberian permafrost, offering new insights into how life endures extreme conditions.

science.title: Cretaceous “Kraken” octopus discovered as apex predator of ancient seas
science.description: Scientists uncover a giant “Kraken-like” octopus that ruled Cretaceous seas, reaching 61 feet and competing with sharks and marine reptiles.
